serving.config_yaml: # extra_llm_api_options — gpt-oss-120b (MXFP4 MoE) on DGX Spark GB10 (sm_121a). # FINAL serving attempt: mirror nemotron's KNOWN-GOOD executor shape as closely as the MXFP4 # arch allows. Prior attempts on this box: # - cuda graphs on (bare default) -> SEGFAULT at batch_size=4 graph capture # - cuda graphs off (eager) + disable_overlap_scheduler:true -> executor DEADLOCK # (server ready, GPU idle ~10%, requests never scheduled, no iter logs) # - harmony 400 fixed separately via DISABLE_HARMONY_ADAPTER=1 (passthrough env) # This config: keep eager (avoid the capture segfault), but add the executor knobs nemotron # runs with (chunked prefill, stream_interval) and DROP disable_overlap_scheduler (a likely # cause of the eager scheduling deadlock). print_iter_log:true so we can SEE if the executor # iterates during a request. kv_cache_config: free_gpu_memory_fraction: 0.8 moe_config: backend: AUTO cuda_graph_config: null enable_chunked_prefill: true stream_interval: 1 print_iter_log: true

model_metadata.role: external agentic anchor + second 4-bit MoE (OpenAI open model, MXFP4, Blackwell-tuned) — replaces DeepSeek as model #3; lets us separate model effects from FP4-on-Blackwell effects alongside Nemotron NVFP4

sanity_check.prompt: Write a Python function `nth_prime(n)` that returns the n-th prime number (1-indexed), with a short docstring and three example assertions. Then explain in two sentences how it works.

fairness_note: Runtime is TensorRT-LLM (vs vLLM for gpt-oss/qwen baselines). Quality metrics are model-driven and comparable across runtimes; throughput/energy/latency are runtime-dependent and must be read with the qwen/gpt-oss dual-runtime bridge. seed, temperature, and the OpenCode version are held identical to the vLLM runs.
